description: Based on comprehensive analysis of Elicit, Consensus, SciSpace, ResearchRabbit, Connected Papers, NotebookLM, Semantic Scholar, and PaperQA2, implement the highest-impact missing features. Focus: augmented reading, evidence synthesis, systematic review workflow, concept graphs, and multi-document analysis.

userStories.title: Add augmented PDF reading with skimming highlights and inline citation cards
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need AI-powered skimming highlights (Goal/Method/Result categories) and inline citation cards when reading PDFs, like Semantic Scholar's Semantic Reader. Currently the PDF reader shows raw PDF with selection-based Q&A but no AI-assisted reading overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/{id}/papers/{paper_id}/highlights returns Goal/Method/Result highlights
GET /projects/{id}/papers/{paper_id}/citation-cards returns citation TLDRs
GET /projects/{id}/papers/{paper_id}/definitions returns term definitions
Frontend highlight overlay with customizable opacity in PDF reader
Inline citation card component showing TLDR on click
Tests: verify highlight endpoint returns structured data
Tests: verify citation card returns TLDR for known papers
Tests: verify definition endpoint returns explanations
userStories.notes: Add augmented_reading_service.py. Use LLM to classify paper sections as Goal/Method/Result. Match citation DOIs against project papers for TLDR cards. Frontend: add overlay layer to PDF reader.

userStories.title: Add evidence consensus visualization for research questions
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to see what percentage of papers support, contradict, or are neutral on a given claim, like Consensus's Consensus Meter. Currently RAG Q&A returns a single answer but no aggregate evidence visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/{id}/rag/evidence-consensus accepts a research question
Returns support_count, contradict_count, mixed_count with percentages
Each paper has a one-line finding extraction
Results include confidence score and source quotes
Frontend EvidenceConsensus component with chart
Tests: verify consensus returns structured data
Tests: verify one-line findings per paper
Tests: verify confidence scores bounded [0,1]
userStories.notes: Add evidence_consensus_service.py. Pipeline: RAG retrieval, LLM classifies stance, extract one-line findings. Frontend: new component in src/components/evidence/.

userStories.title: Add multi-document contradiction detection
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need the system to automatically identify where papers contradict each other, like PaperQA2's ContraCrow. Currently no cross-document conflict analysis exists.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/{id}/analysis/contradictions analyzes all papers
Returns contradiction pairs with: paper_a_id, paper_b_id, claim, positions, confidence
Contradictions grouped by topic/claim
Frontend ContradictionReport component with table
Tests: verify detection finds known conflicts
Tests: verify no false positives on agreeing papers
userStories.notes: Add contradiction_service.py. Extract claims via LLM, group by embedding similarity, compare positions. Cache per project.

userStories.title: Add iterative visual discovery with prior/derivative works
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to trace a paper's intellectual lineage backwards (prior works) and forwards (derivative works), like ResearchRabbit and Connected Papers. Currently only direct citations via Semantic Scholar.
acceptanceCriteria: Citation graph has Prior Works, Derivative Works, and Similarity toggle modes
Prior Works: papers cited by the seed paper
Derivative Works: papers citing the seed paper
Any graph paper can become new seed (iterative)
Similarity edges based on embedding cosine similarity
Tests: verify prior works returns cited papers
Tests: verify derivative works returns citing papers
Tests: verify similarity edges connect related papers
userStories.notes: Enhance citation_graph_service.py for both directions + similarity edges from ChromaDB. Frontend: toggle modes in CitationGraphView.

userStories.title: Add paper collections and smart tagging within projects
userStories.description: As a researcher with 100+ papers, I need to organize them into collections/folders with AI-suggested tags. Currently papers are a flat list.
acceptanceCriteria: New Collection model with CRUD endpoints
POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /projects/{id}/collections
POST /projects/{id}/collections/{cid}/papers to add papers
POST /projects/{id}/tags/suggest returns AI tags per paper
GET /projects/{id}/papers?collection_id=X filter
Frontend: collection sidebar with drag-and-drop
Tests: verify collection CRUD
Tests: verify smart tagging returns tags
userStories.notes: New model: collection.py. New service: collection_service.py. Smart tagging: LLM with title+abstract. Frontend: sidebar panel.

userStories.title: Add systematic review workflow with data extraction tables
userStories.description: As a researcher conducting a systematic review, I need custom extraction columns (sample size, methodology, outcome) and structured data extraction from all papers, like Elicit.
acceptanceCriteria: New Review model with research_question, columns (JSON), paper_ids
CRUD: POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /projects/{id}/reviews
POST /projects/{id}/reviews/{rid}/extract runs LLM extraction
SSE streaming progress for extraction
GET /projects/{id}/reviews/{rid}/export?format=csv downloads CSV
Frontend: review page with column builder and data table
Tests: verify review CRUD
Tests: verify extraction returns structured data
userStories.notes: New model: review.py. New service: review_service.py. Extraction: LLM with paper content + column definitions. Frontend: new /projects/:projectId/reviews route.

userStories.title: Add concept knowledge graph and topic pages
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to see how concepts connect across papers, like Semantic Scholar topic pages. Currently only citation graph exists.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/{id}/concepts/extract identifies key concepts
GET /projects/{id}/concepts/graph returns concept network
Each concept: name, frequency, related_papers, related_concepts, definition
GET /projects/{id}/concepts/{name}/page returns topic overview
Frontend: concept graph with clickable topic pages
Tests: verify concept extraction
Tests: verify concept graph relationships
userStories.notes: Add concept_service.py. Extract concepts via LLM, build graph via co-occurrence. Topic pages: LLM overview.

userStories.title: Add AI library organization with auto-tagging and metadata repair
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need AI to automatically organize papers: fix missing metadata, suggest tags, and group related papers.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /projects/{id}/library/health returns papers with missing metadata
POST /projects/{id}/library/repair fixes missing metadata
POST /projects/{id}/library/auto-tag suggests tags for untagged papers
POST /projects/{id}/library/clusters groups related papers
Tests: verify health identifies missing fields
Tests: verify repair fills metadata
Tests: verify clustering produces coherent groups
userStories.notes: Add library_service.py. Health: scan for null fields. Repair: call Semantic Scholar API. Clustering: embedding K-means.

userStories.title: Add enhanced reading analytics with productivity metrics
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need detailed analytics: papers per week trends, reading streaks, knowledge coverage, citation impact distribution. Currently only basic status counts exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Enhanced analytics: papers_per_week, avg_read_time, reading_streak, domain_coverage, citation_impact
Reading time tracking in PDF reader
Knowledge gap analysis: underrepresented topics
Frontend: enhanced dashboard with all new charts
Tests: verify analytics returns all metrics
userStories.notes: Enhance existing analytics endpoint. Frontend: measure time in PDF reader, report to backend. Use embeddings for topic coverage.

userStories.title: Add personalized research feed with paper recommendations
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need a personalized feed of relevant papers based on reading history and interests, like Semantic Scholar Research Feeds. Currently only keyword subscriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /projects/{id}/feed returns personalized recommendations
Based on: reading history, liked papers, keywords, recent activity
Each: paper metadata, relevance_score, reason
POST /projects/{id}/feed/refresh forces recalculation
POST /projects/{id}/feed/{paper_id}/feedback for learning
Frontend: feed page with card-based infinite scroll
Tests: verify feed returns recommendations
Tests: verify feedback improves recommendations
userStories.notes: Add feed_service.py. Build interest vector from read/liked papers. Search Semantic Scholar/OpenAlex. Score by cosine similarity. Frontend: new feed route.
